free_text: Double-energy single-time-of-flight measurement in
thermal neutron induced fission of 235U.
Experiment performed at thermal
neutron beam of Brookhaven medical reactor
Thin 235U sample with thickness of 30 mu-g/cm2
fixed on Ni backing of thickness 100 mu-g/cm2.
Two solid-state detectors placed in an
evacuated tube. 235U sample placed between two
detectors.
Coincidence between two Si fission fragment
detectors was required.
Mass of fission fragments was determined using time
of flight information from two experimental runs with
different flight paths.
Thermal neutron beam of Brookhaven medical reactor
Compiled by S.H.
